channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: FIRE is mental illness — 154 replies — 616.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
FIRE is mental illness

Open thread • Replies: 154 • Updated: 2026-05-22 16:21 UTC

OP

No woman, no kids, $25k YEARLY spend.
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing. What is wrong with modern men?

Early replies (first 3)

62259938
>>62259935
Keep slaving away goyman

62259945
>>62259935
Nice bait thread faggot

62259968
>>62259935
You can't really afford to marry on FIRE because one divorce ruins a decade+ of saving and then you have to work again. If a woman finds out you're FIRE she will absolutely divorce rape you too since the wealth disparity between you and her is so great she'll walk away with a ludicrous amount of money.

Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62261403
>>62259938
>just rot in you goyburb for 45 years until you die alone

62261427
>>62261134
100 blow jobs in 2 years is actually some kind of record. Even the best blow job queen i ever met preferred to have her pussy fucked than to blow me.

62261430
>>62261104
Didn't his wife take his tens of millions then start fucking her yoga instructor within days?

62261451
This needs to be known as incelFIRE, lonely guys who just want to check out of society and working and live in their cabin in the woods.

62261480
>>62261451
Its impossible to do if you want your kids setup for the future. None of these people talk about private school/college/tutoring and extracurriculars. I would gladly go into debt for that shit if i had kids. these people just want to rot until inflation catches up to them. In which case they need to add 40% to their current projected goal anyways.

62261786
>>62259935
I don't know living in a studio apartment, driving a more than 300k kilometer car and eating on coupon might be a little extreme.
But a two room if living alone, with a second hand but good condition asian car, and buying groceries from standard supermarket seems ok.

62261787
>>62259935
Yeah, maybe men are just tired of being abused and don't want to play in a rigged game any longer. Especially when they can't even be certain the kid is theirs. Best to just check out and let the immigrants take over. If women don't like the world run by browns then maybe they shouldn't have destroyed the family unit.

62261791
>>62259935
sorry but working for a living in an unnatural and hellish existence and i will do everything in my power to escape it as quickly as possible (which will be punctuated by my posting of "nigger" on linkedin with my real name.)

62262016
>>62259935
Why can't women just stop demanding so much?

62262026
Why do poor people think illiquid assets like houses even matter in these calculations?

62262037
>>62259997
I know the exact group in Guangzhou who makes these comics kek.

62262112
>>62259935
>what, why won't you be a slave??
>don't you know women don't like that?

I can't wait for the collapse.

62262198
>>62259935
>But you're not SUPPOSED to do that noooooo you're SUPPOSED to BREED and then WAGESKEK so you can give all your money and resources to WOMEN and be SUBSURVIENT to women!!!! NOOOOOO!!
*cries mountains*

62262206
>you don't want to be a wageslave?
>ha you're a jew!
this board is so fucking retarded sometimes

62262238
>>62259935
Being a wagie sucks. Being independently wealthy and not having to listen to morons is priceless.

62262303
>>62260989
>thinks putting 2.5M in sgov is the best choice for a port that size

62262314
>>62259968
> Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.
Prenups can be pretty ironclad even in gay states about pre-martial assets. Meaning you get to keep all the shares, bonds, coins, and your house if you owned them before. She would be entitled to assets earned DURING the marriage. In a FIRE scenario that’s not going to be a ton, since you’re mostly coasting. If it does turn into a lot of assets, well who cares, you keep your initial balances and half of what you earned.

62262321
>>62259935
> and spend the rest of your life relaxing.
Why not?

62262331
>>62260025
> The judge will look at your net worth and then slap you with an insane child support bill.
That’s not how that works. Are you jewish or just retarded?

62262335
>>62259993
>>62259984
>>62259980
I'm not googling your gay term.
God bots fucking suck.

62262345
>>62260055
> 60k is pretty good money in flyover states
For starting out or early in your career, yeah it’s good. If you’re older than 28-29 I would say it’s not.
>t flyover

62262353
>>62260080
>something something definition of insanity
If someone gets divorced twice and remarry again clearly they’ve got a few screws loose

62262359
>>62262353
i liked that character

62262485
>>62259935
>NO GOY YOU ARE DOING IT WRONG YOU ARE SUPPOSED TO CHASE HOLLOW MATERIAL POSESSIONS, TRAP YOURSELF IN PERMANENT SLAVERY AND PRODUCE FUTURE SLAVES!!1!

How about you go and zuck yourself, Schlomo?

62262639
>>62260231
> The problem is in modern dating its very difficult to pass that first barrier
That’s my struggle. Not going to say I’m some chad, but I’m successful in many aspects. I’m successful with women once I get my foot in the door, but like you said, it’s incredibly difficult in today’s world.

62262648
>>62262485
kek

62262721
>>62259997
Because once you get beyond the "haha I'm traveling bit" it's kinda a shitty place to live. If you're going to be a monk and have a shit apartment and spend your day playing vidya and eating goyslop with your cat, but Panama also involves banging hookers, I guess.

62262729
>>62259980
> net worth will greatly exceed your peers and especially any women in your age bracket.

Yes it's much better to be a poorfag to get married, lmao. How fucking stupid are you? And why the fuck would you marry a working woman who is going to keep working anyways? Who is taking care of your house and homeschooling your kids and baking cookies for the church function?

62262791
>>62261282
I mean, men getting married and reproducing is obviously important on paper. I see why society needs it. But this society doesn’t deserve it. They’ve shit on men for too long. I refuse to even consider marriage until circumcision is outlawed in the USA.

Which will never happen, but that’s fine by me.

62262812
>>62261290
>Notice how I said I’d kms
They won’t let you, retard. You’ll get Baker Acted, and they’ll stuff you into a fun little room with no bed sheets or sharp edges. And you’ll sit there until you give up the gold. The cameras will “malfunction,” and the guards will step into your room and beat the piss out of you. You think you’re the first genius to think of this bullshit?

item.title: HODL and buy more BTC. — 73 replies — 292.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
HODL and buy more BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 73 • Updated: 2026-05-22 16:23 UTC

OP

If you still don’t own any Bitcoin yet, anon what the hell are you waiting for?

>Banks give you tiny interest while inflation slowly destroys your purchasing power anyway. I’d rather have BTC long term than let my money rot in a savings account.

Early replies (first 3)

62245757
i get 5btc later for cheaper but thanks for the advice!

62245760
>>62245741
Too eqrly but yeah and avoid cex or at least take it out as fast as u can!

62246438
>>62245757
How much BTC are you holding right now anon?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62257345
>>62257173
ICp? Lol

62257392
>>62257204
but everything in defi is hype

62257440
>>62257223

62257675
>>62245741
>Banks give you tiny interest while inflation slowly destroys your purchasing power anyway.
BTC doesn't improve any of this. Its cheaper and easier to spend gold.

62257679
>>62246878
It was decided that BTC would never scale. Self custody is impossible on BTC. Go ahead and try it. I swear when I make it I am going to hunt small blockers with a rifle.

62257680
it's boomer coin

62258573
>>62257679
will your cut your d?

62258577
>>62257680
you have zero bitcoin

62258754
>>62257223
Where is he now

62258775
>>62257679
nonsense. It's all in your head

62258793
>>62258754

62259397
>>62245757
lets hold hands and buy in Oct

62259745
>>62259397
What's happening in October?

62260090
>>62259745
He’s buying

62261050
>>62257679
BTC infrastructure is basically trying to solve that balance now: preserving self custody while making native BTC more usable across broader financial systems. Maybe you’re just not updated yet.

62261057
>>62252472
Buy btc for many reasons anon.

62261131
>>62257675
*sends 5 million dollars worth of gold across the world in 5 minutes for a nominal fee*

62261260
>>62261050
It's kinda scary to go deeper with that
>>62261131
Plus big chance for theft

62261264
>>62258793
Hello I'm here

62261340
>>62257392
>>62261260
Nothing wrong with being cautious, just don’t treat all BTCFi the same. Big names like a16zcrypto, Ledger, GoMining and others wouldn’t even touch it if everything was complete garbage.

62261342
>>62252488
50k? Nooo way

62261452
>>62260090
100k by October

62261462
>>62261452
no chance
this will count as the summer of the big dump

62261476
>>62261462
Your opinion doesn't matter to me

62261608
>>62261340
Why do people still not know about this?
Better to explore and understand the TBV direction early before launch.

62261618
>>62261462
How do you know?

62261632
>>62252488
Poor mindset

62261641
>>62261131
Fee: $20000

62262278
>>62259397
Buy altcoins too

62262820
>>62261608
No need to rush. People will figure it out eventually. You can already see more attention shifting toward DeFi outside the usual Ethereum-only lane anyway.

item.title: PACK. A. SANDWHICH. WITH. SOME. CHIPS. — 37 replies — 148.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
PACK. A. SANDWHICH. WITH. SOME. CHIPS.

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-05-22 16:24 UTC

OP

PACK. A. SANDWHICH.

WITH. SOME. CHIPS.

Early replies (first 3)

62262487
>>62262449
maybe don't buy guac if you are poor and it costs $4.90

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62262526
>>62262474
28 dollars on lunch is only 10k a year. 10k a year extra won't buy you a house.

62262535
>>62262449
Culvers isn't fast food its the best burger restaurant in the usa and they're allowed to charge a premium. If you want a cheap burger, go to Crystals.

62262549
>>62262449
This is what I do, I still make $26 a hour or $22 a hour depending on which soul crushing job with zero benefits I'm working. Not gonna get me a house when the median price for one is $550k.

62262577
>>62262449
>>62262454
>>62262474
>>62262487
Why can most people in "poorer countries" like many asian countries afford to eat out every day?

62262587
>>62262577
America doesn't have the infrastructure to make cheap, simple, clean food. Everything has to be standardized in an overpriced restaurant. And if you want to cook something yourself, you have to just break your back cooking for a couple hours or eat just poor food like a sandwich.

Caleb Hammer probably eats out way more and he's a fat rich fuck.

62262598
>>62262474
>Your loaf of bread will last two weeks
I haven't had one last 5 days without going moldy. But then, I buy the ones *without* the ground up gym mat perservatives added.

62262603
>>62262526
It might get you a down payment on a middle class home by the time you're 40. Assuming the middle class home doesn't appreciate.

62262607
I'm conflicted on this debate. On the one hand boomers are retarded and had it immensely easier. You could be a cashier and your purchasing power was greater than a software dev today. So ya, you were able to buy a house and land, and oh ya since you abandoned the gold standard asset prices rose without you having to do any fucking work. So now you have massive assets and your kids are poor, can't buy a home at all, and can only afford Netflix and little Caesars. And you can't figure out why working at Mcdonalds no longer supports a family and you blame Netflix fubs.

On the other hand, poor people who use door dash are legitimately mentally ill.

62262616
>>62262577
The Japanese "Bento Box" is literally home-made lunch. Rice with a few small slices of fish or hot dog in it.

62262627
>>62262577
Regulations, property prices, taxes, zoning laws, supply chain issues, fuel costs, and the minimum wage per wagie is like $60k a year.

62262630
>>62262587
Get a rice cooker, bread maker, and crock pot with at least 2 containers. And a griddle. I have a decently sized eclectic one that is safe in normal outlets if you don't run it hours at a time. It's life changing. I'm eating restaurant quality meals every day that take me 5 min to throw together. I'm eating pounds of steak and it's fucking delicious and costs like $5 a meal. If I used chuck roast I could probably eat all fucking week for $15-20. Rice, beans, bread, eggs, steak. I'm basically on a body building diet but accidently, it just tastes great and is cheap as fuck.

62262632
>>62262616
And we don't have that in America. All the gas station meals and grocery meals are all filled with sodium and fat. Let them eat cake, I guess. Caleb hammer and all the fucking billionaires are hypocritical rich fucks.

62262635
>>62262474
>Chips for lunch
Do mutts really?

62262643
>>62262616
People think sushi and shit is harder than it is. If you have a rice cooker you just buy sushi rice, get some rice vinegar, the sushi sheets, I think it's called nori or something. Some cans of tuna, siracha sauce. You now have unlimited sushi at like $1.50 for a massive roll. And it takes only 5 min of prep. Put rice in cooker. Open tuna. Wrap it up. Done.

62262649
>>62262577
Their food has less calories so it's less valuable therefore cheaper.

62262686
I'm about to get naked as fuck.

62262687
>>62262449
>>62262474
TONY STARK ATE OF A CAVE!
WITH A BAG OF CHIPS!

62262691
>>62262686
Run around the house naked. It's better if you have dogs. They'll get all excited and start barking. They'll be all confused and shit but ready to attack whatever threat has you running around.

62262692
>>62262643
>eating canned tuna as sushi
lol, no.
But yeah, it is common in asian countries to bring food from home and making a rice-and-something dish is not difficult.
>>62262632
We have bento meals for sale at restaurants and grocery stores, with a massive hipster markup. My point is that Japanese bring them from home. A lot of asians bring lunch from home.
On the one hand I kind of understand what they're saying. Eating at home is less expensive than eating out (and Door Dash creates even greater inflation).
On the other their proposed solutions are literally "Well you should eat dirt" and I hate them for that. They can't see the whole problem, they're just rich themselves and want to look down on people.
Like anon said, $28 a day that's 10k a year. (Really, if you're only going out to eat during the work week, it's like $6.5k) Which is great money if you want to buy a house thirty years ago.
I have 150k in the bank and I can't afford a house in commute distance to work that isn't literally rotting and collapsing unless I *also* want to spend 50% of my pre-tax income on the mortgage and insurance and property taxes.
Property Values are outrageous and skyrocketing because the same compound interest that causes increasing returns on their investments is working on the cost side of rent, housing prices and tuition.
There are two bedroom condos for sale near me that were $200k three or four years ago, they're 3-400k now because somebody slapped some paint on the outside and the lack of options drives the price up.

62262715
>>62262691
My mom is here bro...

62262738
>>62262687
kino

62262740
>>62262692
I’ve been eating Korean spicy tuna or just mackerel and warm rice on a small piece of seaweed. I’ll dip it in Korean bean paste. Delicious small dinner. I’ll mix Mexican, American, Korean, Burmese, Japanese, French, into a single meal. Some of the shit I eat I question if I’m the first to ever do it. Not trying to be hipster. Just traveled a bit and learned good shit from each country. But ya, put whatever you want in your sushi. I just gave a cheap example that takes no prep.

62262750
So many poor fat shitters making excuses on why the have to keep eating fast food itt lmao

62262760
>>62262526
It doesn't just stop at $28 lunches. When you add up the $100+ monthly phone bills, car payments, car insurance, funko pops, etc. it all adds up.

62262762
>>62262449
I respect caleb a lot because i think if i was in his shoes id just yell at the guests nonstop

62262769
>>62262762
>I respect [fat kike] a lot

62262773
>>62262607
Stop posting

62262815
>>62262449
imagine working a decent office job, one where you get to use your big smart brain to solve problems. For lunch you decide to fuel your system without absolute trash.
its like putting regular grade in your lamborghini

62262817
>>62262773
Sorry anon. AI is only as powerful as the user. I asked how much a sears cashier would make in the 70’s. It said somewhere around 3-4. While gold fluctuated, for most of the 70’s they could buy an ounce for about 1 week of labor. I’m a software developer and I cannot buy an ounce of gold with 1 week of labor. I can’t buy an ounce of gold with 2 weeks of labor at this point. I’m talking real assets here. You’re comparing currency. A sears cashier could acquire hard assets faster than a software engineer can today.

62262825
>>62262449
Eating a bag of chips and a Sandwich for lunch is how you end up fat like Caleb

item.title: NTDOY — 52 replies — 137.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
NTDOY

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-05-22 16:01 UTC

OP

Currently Nintendo stock is extremely mispriced. All the short-term doom and gloom is priced in, ignoring the company's tremendous long-term upside. While it's not some 10x your money in a week meme coin, at this point it's a sleeper stock to hold you should know about.

Early replies (first 3)

62259388
>>62259371
its not mispriced. they make shitty games for 80+$. no one wants to buy their crap because its shit.

62259405
>NTDOY baggie
KWAB

62259407
>>62259371
not misspriced
no point in shilling your bags here btw
>>62259388
this and console era is coming to an end. Future will be only game stations with steam like platforms. retards still go for the ps5 by some sort of pavlovian reflex. it's just a standardized computer with a standardized controller. Nintendo shined for a while 30 years ago now it has become so cringe i can't stand their adds like wtf is even this shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62259729
>>62259371
My dad works here

62259742
>>62259729
Tell him the shareholders want him to work harder

62259746
I hope it works out for you bro but I don't want to fund what I see as a dying company in a dying market.

62259808
>>62259746
Mid 1980s: people predict video game market dead due to major crash and low-quality games
Late 1980s: people predict video game market dead due to arcade cooldown and home console "fad"
Mid 1990s: people predict video game market dead due to "CD Rom bubble panic" as commercial failures like Atari Jaguar and 3DO make people think industry is imploding
Early 2000s: people predict video game market dead due to 3D games becoming the norm and production budgets exploding
Mid 2000s: people predict video game market dead due to popularity of browser and web-based alternatives
Early 2010s: people predict video game market dead due to widespread popularity of mobile and social media gaming alternatives
Mid/Late 2010s: people predict video game market dead due to massive DLC, microtransactions, and "loot box" gambling dynamics
Early 2020s: people predict video game market dead due to "battle royale bubble" with high concentration in games like Fortnite
2026: random faggot on /biz/ predicts video game market dead because "this time it's different"

62259814
>>62259371
Switch 2 owner here. The new console sold very well due to the positive sentiment created by the Switch 1, and the hardware is certainly most pleasant to hold and see. Other than that, it’s a total disappointment.

>no games, the current lineup is both paltry and boring as fuck
>battery is kill after 2 hours
>storage on the console is rather low
>almost every 3rd party game is a fucking "game key card" that combines the worst of physical (having to carry a cartridge) and the worst of digital (takes the little storage you have)
>hardware self-destructs even if you take great care of it, my left joycon stick looks like it took 15 years of beatings after just a few months (it never happened with anything else I own)

Because of these problems, I’m buying 10 times fewer games on the Switch 2 compared to the Switch 1, and I will not buy a Switch 3 in the future given the more than mediocre treatment we get here.

TL;DR Nintendo gives the illusion of doing well with their numbers, but they’re actually shitting the bed. Disappointing performance will ensue if you are a long term shareholder.

62259828
>>62259814
The console is still in its infancy. Every criticism you have is fixable. New console-seller titles are inevitable. Imagine the Switch 2 Pro with battery and storage upgrades, etc.

62259884
>>62259808
Video games aren't dead. Consoles and reliance on proprietary IPs is. Gaming continues in phones for casuals and PC indies for the hardcore crowd. It's funny because had you invested in phones and social media instead of gaming companies since the early 2010s you'd be massively better off than an investment in Nintendo. Who's talking about "it'll be different this time"?

And anyway, yes, the world is different in 2026 than it was in 1980. Investment is about the future, not staring at previous success and insisting it'll happen again. What is Nintendo doing that could possibly replicate their boom in the 1980s?

62259900
>>62259828
It’s fixable, but will it be fixed? So far, nothing indicates that Nintendo takes the problem seriously, especially with the "no games" problem. Looks like Nintendo will just release four or five decent AAA games through the entire console’s lifespan (one Mario, one Zelda or whatever, and these are not even announced) and then call it a day.

62259983
>>62259371
Why would people be hungry for buying an ever increasing in price console (due to RAM) with zero new Zelda or Mario on it?

62260004
>>62259814
Dude, SW2 is a stop gap filler console until the real one (with a new hardware gimmick) wil be ready, the big games (mainline mario/zelda) will come out very late to be also used as launch titles for the next.
This is wii u/gba/3ds all over again.

62260032
>>62259371
>making this thread again
Please god nuke the third world

62260035
>>62259371
I agree. Not because of vidya, but because of Pokémon cards. The market cap of all PSA10 graded Pokémon cards is over $75B. Nintendo's market cap os only $53B. It makes no sense that the company that makes the cards is worth so much less than the cards themselves.

62260053
>>62260004
Nintendo could actually get away with it (releasing a Switch 3, 4, 5, etc. forever) by not being so retarded and underwhelming… Instead they will be forced to invent something else that may or may not be successful.

62260188
>>62259371
Thanks

62260304
>>62259371
agreed p/e is slurpable, nintendo aint going away

62260335
>>62259884
already priced in

62260390
Apple is apple

62260407
I bought a Rog ALLY X and it’s 100000x better than the switch I had for my son. You know the target audience? My kid uses the Ally to watch YouTube play actual fun games. He cares more about Spider-Man than bing bing wahoo slop. Nintendo failed to capture the audience by marketing to childless manchildren just like Disney Adults. They have no market share and sell an inferior product. Pokémon still looks and plays like an indie game from 2005. (lol)

Enjoy your bags tendie you get what you deserve.

62260606
>>62260407

62261314
Sorry about your hopes of pumping your bags OP, but I'll just leave this here...
>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=luiKV1nHB1w

62261355
>>62259371
The switch 2 is overpriced. People who have money don't even know what it is. It also demotes the value of the switch one, which partially went subscription based model. So it was bad business design.

62261416
Tell me the last time they ever release a ground breaking software or hardware? The new pokemon is not a cashgrab.

62261518
anyway, you know when a retard here shill some stock:
1/ he is clueless no amount of shiling here will pump it not even 0.01%

2/therefore it's a bad pick

it's cringe.last retard shilled mc donald like it was some secret gem. The real blackpill is whatever gets shilled here don't buy it or even better sell it if you have some (pokemon cards, random crypto that miraculously pumped x10, nintengoy stocks, mc goynald stock, whatever)

62261624
>>62260035
>It makes no sense that the company that makes the cards is worth so much less than the cards themselves.
What psychogenic kush are you smoking? It makes a ton of sense. By your logic, a goldsmith can't have created a masterpiece work that he has sold 20 years ago but which is now recognized to be more valuably than his entire shop.

62261625
Value Trap 2: Electric Boogaloo

62261640
>>62259371
I agree. They are the one last big truly JP dev remaining, huge franchise value that's only getting better, the only one console with exclusives, ninety-something % of the serious handheld business with proper customer attachment, JP approach to quality as opposed to deadlines, no DEI, success with theme park now expanding, huge success with the films now.
No reason not to own their stock, I think it's a good 50% undervalued.

62261642
>2009
>Nintendo has suffered a drawdown of 50%
>ZOMG invest in Nintendo!!
>keeps dropping
>10 years to reach the position you entered

Yeah nah, I know a correction when I see it.

62261677
>>62261625
kek.
What is your average OP?
Buying this above 2 is like giving to a charity

62262033
>>62261314
Already priced in

62262739
6months performance : - 45%
1 month perfomance : -12%
today's perfomance : - 0.47%

wow troontendgoy really IS daw new millionaire maker!

item.title: Anon, its my bday today Im in early 30s, close to no friends, no gf (ditched a bitch around 2 years ago), not much in th… — 23 replies — 92.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anon, its my bday today Im in early 30s, close to no friends, no gf (ditched a bitch around 2 years ago), not much in th…

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-05-22 16:21 UTC

OP

Anon, its my bday today
Im in early 30s, close to no friends, no gf (ditched a bitch around 2 years ago), not much in the bank, had a successful career but then pivoted to make a startup and lost shit ton of money and time in the process. Not sure what to do anymore. Basically going through the dark night of the soul.

Now I'm here trying to find a tech job in a fucking US to not die on the street. You are, as usual, my only real friend.

Tell me something nice or not.

Early replies (first 3)

62261420
>>62261414
>Now I'm here trying to find a tech job in a fucking US
Are you a foreigner? Is this a LARP?

62261422
You did good

62261428
>>62261414
Your life can turn around in a week easy. New job, new girl, market pumps, whatever. Don't be so down anon you don't know what is around the corner. Also you're only early 30s, you're young as fuck, you have to live as long as you already have all over again before you hit mid 60s, just think how long that truly is. Stop worrying and enjoy your life you fag.

Happy Birthday, enjoy the magic trick.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62261442
>>62261428
whoops wrong file

62261445
Doing way better than I am

62261472
>>62261420
yes im from EU

62261490
>>62261428
thanks anon, i will try my best, there is nothing to do for me anyway

62261501
>>62261414
the answer lies at the end of a shotgun barrel.

You know this, i know this, we all know this yet we cling to life like ticks

62261504
>>62261472
Go back to your country

62261512
>>62261504
I really don’t understand why an euro would want to move to USA these days. T. An euro

62261523
>>62261512
not being paid 20 euros per hour max?

62261539
>>62261512
We have much lower barriers to starting a business or making money.

other than that, yeah. US isn't having a great time right now.

62261551
>>62261539
>We have much lower barriers to starting a business

I don’t know if this was always a myth, but at least nowdays in many european countries it’s just as easy. The only reason why europeans don’t is because we don’t need to thanks to the welfare.

>>62261523
Unless you really luck out, all the extra income goes towards things europeans get for free.

62261563
>>62261551
really doubt it. Engineers here make 2-3k /month they can get 150k in the us. Some people get much more. That's commie cope

62261586
>>62261563
In my country they make 3k at start and over 4k easily, and of course they can get bigger salaries too. It’s a small number of engineers who get 150k in US. Overall there really isn’t an improvement in life quality, and you would have to leave all your friends and family.

62261587
>>62261414
Happy birthday anon. The world is unfair and many who have been successful are finding themselves in dire straits due to how awful everything is.
I think there is always the chance for things to the upside and have faith in you. Good luck.

62262137
At least you tried.

62262164
>>62261490
>>62261501
Cmon now, don't encourage suicide Baldie schizo, I'm the sick-anon, I'm gonna be honest I was not in a good way and you saying that kinda shit wasn't helping, luckily my good friend and beloved companion in this bullshit we call life was able to lift my spirits.
But cmon now even if OP is a faggot, it's easier to be nice than it is to be mean because anger reflects back onto you.
Even you were worried I killed myself when I was trying to sell my guns, yes I do own guns so watch who you say that kinda shit to brother.
I say this for your own sake, while I slept you worried even if it wasn't your focus you did worry.
It was me samefagging as you btw.
I was gonna crank it to 11 until everyone hated you but I backed off, I've ran many people off this board before by copying their style/namefag and annoying people on purpose.

62262734
>>62261414
Try and relax and enjoy your bday
I can relate but not that bad. Seems u had it rough. Hard times pass.
Do something you wanna do today

62262752
>>62262164
im sorry but freedom of speech always come first. If you're going to blow your brain because of a 4 chan anon comment you were likely to do it anyway without it. this is a peculiar joy to saying what the fuck you want to say.

also you can't run me out of this board, we are probably having convo in other thread without even realizing it, since we are max 10 to 20 regular posters here

62262796
>>62261586
It's only low iq third worlders who think American salary is all that matters. EU quality of life is so much better

62262803
>>62261414
30y old.
You are baby.
Keep calm, enjoy your life

62262809
>>62262752
>I'm le victim
No just stop being an asshat. You're free to say what you want and we can call you douchebag with our own freedom of speech.

item.content:encoded:
Oil bulls are coping and seething pretty hard today.

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-22 16:23 UTC

OP

Oil bulls are coping and seething pretty hard today. This is what happens when you bet against the USA - crying in the casino.

Early replies (first 3)

62262308
>>62262296
Moralizing about USgov not allowing oil to moon which is apparently "right" is just amazing

62262381
>>62262308
Yes, it is right as it prevents shortages from happening.

62262518
>>62262296
Seeing oilbullfags claiming media manipulation is both hilarious and infuriating.
They bullshit more than any other market including the crypto shitcoin casino

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62262525
>>62262381
lying doesn't change the reality of supply and demand, it just screws the goyim who still believe in """fundamentals""" and detaches markets from material reality. soon all you have left is a soviet tier economy where market activity is dictated by government mandate

62262540
>>62262525
>Man who gets his worldview from these guys wants us to hear about his "fundamentals"
Lol. Lmao.

62262541
>>62262518
This as well
apparently only the DUMPs are manipulation
All pumps are natural and blessed by the anal juice from the All Mighty himself

62262568
>>62262541
lastly, look at plebbbit
Try posting a link to an article about the war ending and you'll probably get banned
Oil bulls are insufferable

62262656
>>62262540
>ideologically subverted golem argues with strawman in his head

62262703
>>62262308
>>62262381
Oil is already almost double the price it was before. How delulu are people who think it should be at 150-200? Thats literally impossible with the state of the global economy. A recession would drag it dowen immediately. I said since months 100 is the fair price for a close straight and it remains true.

62262713
>>62262525
>reality of supply and demand
Supply AND demand. Demand dropped massively, especially is asia. Its also beeing substituted. Supply didnt even decrease much, Saudi Arabia just sells via pipelines now. Usa massively increased production. Russia massively increased export.

62262725
>>62262308
The oil FUD causes there to be less effort put into conservation of oil and also less effort put into increasing production of oil

62262741
>>62262713
Demand dropped by 2.5 million barrels, supply by 7 million barrels.

62262747
>>62262713
>Saudi Arabia just sells via pipelines now.
The Saudi pipeline is capped at 7mbpd. The UAE pipeline is capped at 1.8mbpd.

> Usa massively increased production
This is not true. US crude production has remained flat. In fact, the US is currently producing slightly less crude than it did earlier this year
https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCRFPUS2&f=W

>Demand dropped massively
Well obviously; demand must match supply (counting stockpiling and stock drawndowns), so if there is less supply, then demand destruction has to occur somewhere

62262755
>>62262745
>>62262747
the usa doesn't want oil to go up, case closed! Buy AI stocks and semi-conductors if you want an easy time making money. These are the things the USA wants to go up.

62262805
>>62262713
>Russia massively increased export.
I don't think this is true either. Russia's export of crude oil has remained approximately flat. Russia's export of refined oil product has declined somewhat due to disruptions to refinery operations caused by drone attacks.

https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/russian-seaborne-oil-product-exports-fell-april-amid-drone-attacks-data-industry-2026-05-15/

62262822
>>62262296
Nothing wrong with a small position in oil stocks but being a straight up oil baggie and essentially betting against the global economy is straight up retardation

item.title: If Ethereum was founded by the same guy who made Polkadot, what is stopping DOT from going to quadruple digits? — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
If Ethereum was founded by the same guy who made Polkadot, what is stopping DOT from going to quadruple digits?

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-22 16:20 UTC

OP

If Ethereum was founded by the same guy who made Polkadot, what is stopping DOT from going to quadruple digits?

Early replies (first 3)

62262358
First mover advantage. You can't just copy a good idea and expect the copy to do as well as the original.

62262548
>>62262291
What argument is this even?

62262550
>>62262291
I'm happy I chose to buy a bunch of polkadot when it was dirt cheap. I believe it has some potential. Idgaf about usecase or whatever. Lines gonna go up.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62262561
>>62262291
Gayvin Wood ! Polkadot's sole purpose is to say "Look Vitalik, I have my own blockchain"

62262609
>>62262291

62262618
>>62262609
And that wouldn't be impossible

62262653
>>62262618
oh honey

62262786
>>62262291

62262792
>>62262291

62262804
>>62262291

item.title: Hello traders and fellow entrepreneurs, I’m excited to show you my robot: NextBotCandle, an automated trading bot develo… — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Hello traders and fellow entrepreneurs, I’m excited to show you my robot: NextBotCandle, an automated trading bot develo…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-22 16:19 UTC

OP

Hello traders and fellow entrepreneurs,

I’m excited to show you my robot: NextBotCandle, an automated trading bot developed for the MetaTrader 5 and MetaTrader 4 ecosystems. The bot is designed to execute trades automatically based on customizable candle sequence conditions, helping traders streamline their strategies and reduce manual intervention.

KEY FEATURES:

> Fully customizable candle sequence logic based on your preferred timeframe
> Adjustable lot size settings
> Configurable Take Profit (TP) and Stop Loss (SL) levels in points
> Automatic trade execution shortly before the final candle closes (approximately 0.5–1 second before completion), which may help reduce spread-related entry disadvantages
> Designed for traders seeking faster and more precise executions

The bot has not yet been published on the MQL5 Market, and I would greatly appreciate any advice or recommendations regarding the best way to launch and promote it there

Thank you in advance for your feedback and support.

- ZV

Early replies (first 3)

62262736
>>62262446
>designed
>developed
Have you tested in real trading? No? Go do that and come back with the data.

62262744
>>62262736
Hello. Yes, I did in fact test it with DEMO accounts. I can share you some clips. DM me through my Telegram (realCapiKev)

62262768
great, hopefully you can make 300$ over a few years with that magnificient algo

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62262778
>>62262768
Greetings. FYI, this isn't any "magical" robot. It's instructions-based

62262785
>>62262744
>DEMO accounts
If you aren't willing to put real money on the table why would anyone else?

62262797
>>62262785
I'm starting with little, but it works

item.title: BREAKING: Ripple Joins Chainlink's SCALE Program — 14 replies — 6.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
BREAKING: Ripple Joins Chainlink's SCALE Program

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-05-22 14:20 UTC

OP

>Ripple is thrilled to announce its participation in Chainlink’s SCALE program, a significant step toward enhancing blockchain interoperability and fostering innovation in financial services.
>By joining SCALE, Chainlink will contribute to the Ripple ecosystem, facilitating seamless access to real-time data and off-chain computation for smart contracts.
>Ripple’s recently launched stablecoin, $RLUSD, will also benefit from enhanced oracle connectivity, enabling greater transparency and reliability for its use in payment and settlement solutions within the TradFi ecosystem.

https://chainlinktoday.com/ripple-joins-chainlink-scale-with-all-chainlink-services-live-on-mainnet/

https://x.com/Ripple/status/1869461289273615724

How much can you invest into tax free portfolios in your country?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-22 14:41 UTC

OP

How much can you invest into tax free portfolios in your country? (not including pensions)
>UK
20k per year

Early replies (first 3)

62261497
>>62261457

$24,500 in Roth 401k
>USA

There are others but I'm not looking it up.

62261502
>>62261457
bold of you to assume that the uk wont simply change the rules once enough brown communists are in parliament

62261505
>>62261457

0

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62261507
>>62261457
All portfolios are tax-free in my country, lol

62261513
>>62261457
>UK
>20k per year
Plus £9,000 in Junior ISA's.

62261530
>>62261497
>$24,500 in Roth 401k
I said not including pensions

62261532
>>62261507
Which country?

62261534
>>62261530
401ks aren't pensions. They're fundamentally different.

62262204
>>62261534
Ok, I should have said not including retirement plans

62262371
>>62261457
in Brazil the government sometimes would let you invest in fund with some of your social security money... in a fund that holds 1 stock and has 2% management fee

it's all so tiresome

62262392
>>62262371
What's the stock?

item.title: WE ARE IN A NEW PARADIGM — 8 replies — 4.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
WE ARE IN A NEW PARADIGM

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-22 14:38 UTC

OP

The line can't go down anymore.

>65%+ of transactions in equity markets are done through passive trading, in indexes tied to marketcap, or through very simple algorithmic trading that outright ignores information and follows simple (if it greens, buy it; if it reds, short it) automatic trades
>that means that a big company can only get bigger, that short squeezes can create entire rallies (like the ones we are seeing), and there is literally no way for the market to crash unless people decide to withdraw their money from ETFs (spoiler: they won't and they can't control it, trillions of dollars in indexed options come from people's 401k(s))
>revenues, profits, costs, supply lines, it all does not matter, all that matters is marketcap because the modern market is outright rigged to ignore balance sheets and just see stock prices

This is a new paradigm of the market, and the only way to make a profit is if you buy, buy, buy everything. Take debt (it frees you from some tax obligations, so it is even better), if necessary, but keep on buying. That is what Ellison is doing, that is what Fink and Musk have realized.

Stocks from the major companies have turned into Cryptocurrency, and should be piled and reserved as such. Unlike Bitcoin, Nvidea stocks even have inherent value, earn dividends, and are far easier to trade.

Even treasuries are losing to AI. Soon, you'll have options to earn your salary in AI tokens.

Early replies (first 3)

62258704
>>62258636
>boomers retiring
>jobs lost to AI
What now?

62258760
>>62258636
Okay saaar boat in with my 1million rupies lifesavings madarchid, only up 100k eoy wagmi lfg oooooooo blodybasterds!

62258815
>>62258636
OP would be telling everyone to buy Cisco and Microsoft stock in mid-2000

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62258909
>>62258815
Nope.

This time it really is different.

62260889
>if we all pretend the economic is booming it means the economy is booming!

truly the collapse can't come soon enough

62260990
>>62258636
I see babby finally learned the capm

62262357
>>62258636
>unless people decide to withdraw their money from ETFs (spoiler: they won't and they can't control it, trillions of dollars in indexed options come from people's 401k(s))
That's a nice way of saying that if something breaks everyone's 401ks get liquidated. Nothing to worry about ofc because the bots will only see red and short the collapse so the right people will still make money.

62262379
>>62258704
extend 401ks to american aligned countries

62253228
>>62252856

>>62253228
